summary: Question 1.1 asks for which K every red/blue-coloring of E^n contains a red unit-distance pair or a blue isometric copy of K; the source paper resolves this for K = ℓ_m (m collinear unit-spaced points) up to the constant in the exponent 2^(cn). Conlon–Wu (2022) extends the analysis to asymmetric variants involving ℓ_3 vs. ℓ_m colorings, and Führer–Tóth (2024) sharpens those explicit bounds from m = 10^50 down to m = 1177. The question for general K beyond collinear configurations remains open.

since_posted.title: More on lines in Euclidean Ramsey theory
since_posted.claim: Proves that for every n there exists a red/blue-coloring of ℝⁿ with no red copy of ℓ₃ and no blue copy of ℓ_m for some explicit m, extending the lines-case analysis of Question 1.1 to asymmetric ℓ_3 vs. ℓ_m variants.

since_posted.title: Progressions in Euclidean Ramsey theory
since_posted.claim: Improves the explicit threshold in the Conlon–Wu coloring construction from m = 10^50 to m = 1177, giving a sharper quantitative answer for the lines/progressions case of the question.

search_queries: Conlon Fox "Euclidean Ramsey theory" lines chromatic number unit distance arxiv 1705.02166
Euclidean Ramsey theory "red/blue coloring" unit distance isometric copy follow-up 2019 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024
notes: Question 1.1 is a broad meta-question rather than a single conjecture; the source paper itself partially answers it for K = ℓ_m (collinear progressions). Follow-up papers address line/progression subsets and improve quantitative bounds, but the question for general K (spherical sets, non-collinear configurations, arbitrary point sets) remains largely open.
